title: 10 best herbal teas for red eyes
intro_desc: Herbal teas for red eyes are infusions made from plants that are known to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated eyes. These teas typically contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that help alleviate the symptoms of red eyes, such as dryness, itchiness, and swelling. Some examples of herbal teas that are beneficial for red eyes include chamomile, calendula, and eyebright. Chamomile tea is well-known for its calming and anti-inflammatory properties, making it an ideal choice for soothing irritated eyes. Calendula tea, on the other hand, contains antioxidants that help reduce redness and inflammation. Eyebright tea is specifically formulated for eye health, and it has been used for centuries to treat various eye conditions, including red eyes. By drinking these herbal teas, individuals can experience relief from red eyes, which can significantly improve their quality of life, especially for those who spend long hours staring at screens or work in dry environments.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garden orach teas helps with red eyes because of its unique ability to soothe and calm irritated tissues. The herbal tea's anti-inflammatory properties, derived from the plant's leafy greens, work wonders in reducing swelling and discomfort caused by allergies, fatigue, or dryness. Additionally, the tea's mild astringent qualities help to constrict blood vessels, which can reduce the appearance of redness and puffiness, leaving eyes looking brighter and more refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Orach tea contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes caused by allergic reactions or irritations.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Orach tea, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to red eye symptoms.

Antihistamine properties: Orach tea contains compounds that have been shown to exhibit antihistamine effects, which can help relieve itching, redness, and discomfort associated with allergic reactions or conjunctivitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe and reduce redness associated with irritated eyes.
Seeds: Containing omega-3 fatty acids and flavonoids, seeds work to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh orach leaves at dawn or just before sunrise for optimal flavor and aroma.
Rinse the harvested orach leaves with pure water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the cleaned orach leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife or scissors.
Steep one teaspoon of chopped orach leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release flavors.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and serve warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beetroot teas helps with red eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in beetroot have been shown to reduce inflammation and congestion, which can cause redness and irritation. Additionally, beetroot's vasodilating effects help to dilate blood vessels, reducing puffiness and discoloration. By soothing and calming the eyes, beetroot tea can provide instant relief from redness and discomfort, leaving eyes feeling cool, calm, and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Beetroot tea's high antioxidant content helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to redness and irritation.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: The polyphenols and flavonoids present in beetroot tea have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and discomfort.
Vasodilatory effects: Beetroot's nitrates can dilate blood vessels, including those around the eyes, which may help reduce redness and puffiness by improving circulation and reducing congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in soothing red eyes by reducing swelling and irritation.
Bark: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with red eyes.
remedy_recipe: Wash 2-3 fresh or dried beetroot roots with cold water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the washed beetroot into small pieces or crush them in a mortar and pestle.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the chopped beetroot in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large bowl to remove solids.
Let the tea cool and then refrigerate it for at least 30 minutes before serving chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea teas helps with red eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The antioxidants present in these teas, such as flavonoids and catechins, work to soothe and calm irritated eye tissues, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, the gentle warmth of the tea can help to relax the muscles surrounding the eyes, further alleviating puffiness and swelling. As a result, white tea teas provide a natural and effective remedy for relieving red eyes caused by fatigue, allergies, or dry air.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant-rich flavonoids: White tea contains high levels of antioxidant-rich flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of redness and irritation.

Anti-inflammatory properties of catechins: White tea's high concentration of catechins has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from discomfort and itchiness.

Soothing properties of astringent compounds: The astringent compounds present in white tea have a drying effect on the eyes, which can help to reduce excess tear production and alleviate irritation, leading to a more comfortable and less red appearance.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Bark: Containing tannins and flavonoids, bark has astringent properties that can reduce redness and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Source high-quality camellia sinensis leaves from reputable suppliers.
Measure out one teaspoon of loose-leaf white tea per eight ounces water.
Heat filtered water to a temperature of 150 to 160 degrees fahrenheit for brewing.
Steep the tea leaves in hot water for exactly five minutes and forty-five seconds.
Strain the tea leaves using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a warmed teapot.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goosefoot teas helps with red eyes because of its unique combination of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, work to soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and puffiness. Additionally, goosefoot contains antihistamines that help alleviate itchiness and discharge associated with red eye conditions like conjunctivitis. As a result, drinking goosefoot tea may provide natural relief from discomfort and promote healthy vision.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Goosefoot tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to redness and discomfort.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in goosefoot tea help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate red eye symptoms.
Astringent properties: Goosefoot's astringent compounds help constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling in the eyes, which can alleviate redness and promote faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Bark: Contains antioxidants and flavonoids that reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried chenopodium album leaves and stems according to your needs.
Rinse the harvested material with cold water to remove dirt debris and impurities thoroughly.
Chop or crush the material into small pieces to release essential oils and flavor compounds completely.
Steep one teaspoon of chopped goosefoot in 1 cup of boiling water for 10-15 minutes carefully.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids before serving immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright teas helps with red eyes because it contains soothing and anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and irritation. The tea's flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have natural antihistamine properties that help to alleviate redness and itching caused by allergens or irritants. Additionally, eyebright's mucilage coats the eyes with a protective film, reducing dryness and discomfort associated with red eyes. This natural remedy provides fast and effective relief from red, itchy, or tired eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Eyebright tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, alleviating redness and discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in eyebright tea, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to eye irritation, promoting overall eye health and reducing the appearance of redness.

Soothing mucilage: Eyebright tea contains a type of soothing mucilage that helps protect and moisturize the eyes, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with red eyes by providing a protective barrier against environmental irritants.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used to soothe and calm irritated eyes, roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and swelling.
Flowers: Rich in bioactive compounds, flowers are used to create a mild and non-irritating tea that helps to reduce eye inflammation and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried euphrasia officinalis flowers into a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Bring fresh cold water to a boil in a kettle or pot then pour it over the herb mixture.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-7 minutes depending on your desired strength and flavor profile.
Strain the tea into another cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth if necessary.
Serve the eyebright tea hot or iced with a slice of lemon and honey to taste.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with red eyes because its natural antihistamine properties can effectively soothe and calm irritated conjunctiva, reducing inflammation and alleviating itchiness. The tea's flavonoids also help to strengthen blood vessels in the eye, improving circulation and promoting healthy tear production. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce redness and swelling by neutralizing free radicals that can exacerbate eye irritation. By addressing these underlying causes, herbal fennel teas provide a natural solution for soothing red eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can reduce swelling and ease eye discomfort, alleviating redness.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in fennel tea helps protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to redness and irritation.
Sothative: Fennel's soothing properties, due to its anethole compound, can calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and discomfort.
Leaves: Fennel leaves contain antioxidants and flavonoids that have natural anti-allergic properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and promoting eye health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried fennel leaves and stems in the morning when they are at their most potent and fragrant.
Measure out one tablespoon of dried fennel or two tablespoons of fresh fennel for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Heat the water to a boil in a large pot then reduce the heat to a simmer for five to seven minutes.
Add the measured fennel to the pot and let it steep for an additional three to five minutes depending on desired strength.
Strain the tea into a cup or teapot using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and serve hot.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with red eyes because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed as a tea, it can help to reduce puffiness and soothe irritated eyes caused by allergies, dryness, or other factors. The flavonoids present in licorice tea also have antioxidant effects, which can help to protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage and promote healthy vision. As a result, drinking herbal licorice tea may provide relief for red, itchy, or irritated eyes, leaving them feeling calm and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, alleviating redness and discomfort.
Antioxidants: Herbal licorice tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, further reducing redness and irritation.
Soften and moisturize: The saponins present in licorice root can help soften and moisturize the delicate tissues of the eyes, reducing dryness and irritation that can contribute to red eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated eyes.
Bark: The bark contains flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to alleviate redness and discomfort in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried licorice root powder per cup of boiling water.
Combine the licorice root powder with water in a medium-sized saucepan and bring to boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Allow the tea to cool for 2-3 minutes before serving hot or at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel teas helps with red eyes because it possesses anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that effectively soothe and calm irritated eyes. The tea's natural astringent properties also help to reduce swelling and inflammation, while its antimicrobial properties combat infection and eliminate the root cause of the redness. As a result, witch hazel teas provide quick relief from red eyes caused by allergies, dry air, or other irritants.
remedy_properties: Astringent property: Witch hazel's astringent property helps to constrict blood vessels and reduce inflammation in the eye, which can alleviate redness and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in witch hazel tea may help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the eyes, making it an effective remedy for red, irritated eyes.

Antioxidant property: Witch hazell's antioxidants can help to protect the eyes from further damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic eye conditions like dry eye or conjunctivitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Witch hazel roots contain a high concentration of tannins, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues in the eyes.
Bark: The bark of the witch hazel plant contains salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin that helps to alleviate pain and reduce swelling in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh witch hazel leaves and twigs, then rinse them thoroughly with clean water.
Combine one teaspoon of dried witch hazel leaves with one quart of boiling water, steep for ten minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids and sediment.
Discard the solids and store the tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.
Pour two cups of the cooled tea into a cup and sweeten with honey if desired, enjoy warm or chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common sunflower teas helps with red eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease irritation. The antioxidants present in sunflowers, such as vitamin E and beta-carotene, help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to redness. Additionally, sunflower petals contain flavonoids, which have been shown to have a soothing effect on the eyes and reduce puffiness. Drinking sunflower tea can provide gentle relief from red, itchy, or irritated eyes, making it a natural remedy for occasional eye discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Sunflower tea contains flavonoids and polyphenols, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate redness in the eyes, making it an effective natural treatment for red eye relief.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in sunflower tea help neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes and contribute to conditions like conjunctivitis (pink eye), thus reducing redness and discomfort.

Vasodilatory properties: Sunflower tea's flavonoids may also help dilate blood vessels, which can reduce the appearance of redness in the eyes by promoting healthy circulation and relaxation of the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, sunflower roots help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Seeds: Sunflower seeds contain essential fatty acids that reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sunflower petals from mature flowers with no pesticides or contaminants.
Measure one tablespoon of dried sunflower petals or two teaspoons of fresh petals for every cup of boiling water.
Steep the sunflower petals in boiling water for five to seven minutes to release flavonoids and antioxidants.
Strain the tea mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl or individual cups.
Enjoy warm or cooled tea immediately, storing any leftovers in an airtight container for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roselle teas helps with red eyes because it contains a potent antioxidant called anthocyanin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed as a tea, roselle's anthocyanins work to soothe and calm irritated blood vessels in the eyes, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy circulation. This natural remedy also has a gentle decongestant effect, helping to reduce puffiness and redness associated with eye strain, allergies, or sinus pressure, leaving your peepers feeling refreshed and comfortable.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Roselle tea is rich in antioxidants such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of redness.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like anthraquinones and saponins, which can help to reduce swelling and congestion in the eyes, relieving redness and discomfort.

Vasodilatory properties: Roselle tea's flavonoids and other compounds can help to dilate blood vessels, reducing pressure on the eyes and improving circulation, which can further alleviate redness and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the roselle plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and soothing red, itchy eyes.
Bark: The bark of the roselle plant contains compounds that help to calm and cool irritated eyes, providing relief from redness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calyces of hibiscus sabdariffa flowers for optimal flavor and nutritional benefits.
Rinse the calyces with clean water to remove any dirt or debris and retain their natural potency.
Combine 1 cup of calyces with 8 cups of boiling water in a large pot and let steep for 10-15 minutes to release flavors.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to separate the solids from the liquid, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool before serving and enjoy as a refreshing and caffeine-free beverage with potential health benefits.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat red eyes with herbal teas is by using chamomile or green tea bags. Steep the tea bags in hot water for several minutes, then let them cool down until they're comfortably warm. Gently place the tea bags over your closed eyes for 10-15 minutes. Both chamomile and green tea contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and irritation. Remember to always test the temperature of the tea bags before applying them to your eyes to avoid burns. If redness persists, consult a healthcare professional.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to red eyes that are treated with herbal teas include dry eye, eye strain, and conjunctivitis. Chamomile tea is a popular choice for its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help alleviate redness and irritation. Green tea, rich in antioxidants, can reduce eye puffiness and dark circles. For dry eye, fennel or eyebright tea can provide relief by increasing tear production and reducing inflammation. When using herbal teas for eye ailments, it is important to ensure the tea is properly cooled and to avoid using tea bags that have been previously steeped, as they can harbor bacteria. Always consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
